Hayes Valley · San Francisco

Dinner, by firelight

A seasonal restaurant cooking the day's market over oak and almond wood — low light, a short natural-wine list, and a hearth you can sit beside.

100%
Whole-animal

Our story

A market stall and a borrowed grill, grown into a room lit by its own fire.

We cook almost everything over live oak and almond wood — the flavor a wood fire gives you can't be faked with a dial. Bread is milled and baked in-house and vegetables arrive twice a week from growers we know by name.

We use the whole animal so nothing is wasted. No dress code, no theatre — just low light, careful cooking, and people who want to feed you well.

— Mara & the Sorrel kitchen

The menu · this week

Written by the fire

Hearth bread & cultured butter

SnacksStone-milled levain, baked to order, smoked sea salt.

Charred little gems

GardenAnchovy, aged sheep's cheese, toasted breadcrumb.

Hand-cut tagliatelle

PastaSlow ragù of the day, brown butter, garden herbs.

Whole fish on the coals

FireLine-caught, grilled over oak, salsa verde, lemon.

Dry-aged beef, shared

FireLocal ranch, embers, bone marrow, watercress.

Olive-oil cake

SweetsRoasted stone fruit, crème fraîche, honey.
Book the hearth menu
Why Sorrel

Four things we keep sacred

Live-fire kitchen

Almost everything touches oak or almond wood. No gas line, no shortcuts.

Market-led

The menu is rewritten daily around what local farms pick that morning.

Natural wine

A short, living list of low-intervention bottles. Ask and we'll pour.

A room with low light

Walk-ins welcome at the bar. No dress code, no theatre.

How a night works

From booking to the last bite

STEP 01

Reserve online

Book a table or counter seat through our reservations partner. Walk-ins welcome at the bar.

STEP 02

Come hungry

Arrive, settle in with a glass. Tell us about allergies — we'll build the night around you.

STEP 03

Let the kitchen lead

Order à la carte, or hand it over for the hearth menu and let us send the best of today.

Wood-fired ✦ Market-led ✦ Natural wine ✦ Baked in-house ✦  Wood-fired ✦ Market-led ✦ Natural wine ✦ Baked in-house ✦

The reel

Inside Sorrel

Good to know

Questions

Do you take walk-ins?+
Yes — the bar and a handful of counter seats are kept for walk-ins every night. The dining room books up, so reserve ahead for a table at a set time.
Allergies & dietary needs?+
Always. Tell us when you book or sit down. Because we cook to the market we can flex most dishes, with strong vegetable and gluten-free options nightly.
Is there a tasting menu?+
Yes — the Hearth Menu is a chef's selection of the day's best, served family-style. Book the counter to watch it come together.
Do you do corkage?+
We do, for a per-bottle fee, on bottles not on our list. Share a taste with the team and it's on us.
Where should I park?+
Street parking around Hayes Valley plus the Civic Center garage two blocks east. Short walk from Van Ness Muni; a bike rack out front.

Private dining & events

We'll cook across the city

Buy out the room or bring the hearth to you — dinners and events around the Bay:

Hayes ValleyNoPaThe MissionPacific HeightsMission BayOakland

Credentials

Run the right way

  • SF DPH health rating: A
  • Certified SF Green Business
  • Fully licensed & insured bar
  • Good Food Purchasing member
